title: Consumer Lifecycle, Cancellation, Readiness, and Graceful Shutdown · Type-System Trace: Cancel Inflight Boundary
summary: A consumer reports ready before consume-ok, ignores broker cancellation, closes the connection while handlers run, and assumes cancel immediately stops every in-flight delivery. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cancel inflight boundary increment in consumer registration, readiness, and shutdown state machine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Cancel new deliveries, then await, finish, nack, or abandon in-flight work under a deadline before closing the channel.
Handle a null delivery callback and cancellation notifications as lifecycle events with cause, readiness, recovery, and ownership evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Cancel Inflight Boundary · Theory Cancel Inflight Boundary
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 6552 in a partner publisher using an unroutable changed topic key. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to sequence readiness, cancellation, in-flight draining, deadline, and channel close; preserve one tenant fairness invariant; then make the broker confirmation disappear after the message may have committed. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Cancel Inflight Boundary contract for the supplied data.
